reforms.description: The proposed budget for 1980 foresees a reduction in selective support schemes to industry, which had previously increased due to the recession, aiming to improve productivity and structural adjustment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reduction in selective support schemes is intended to enhance productivity and facilitate structural adjustments in the industry.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposed budget for 1980 foresees a reduction in selective support schemes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment in support mechanisms aimed at improving industry productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The proposed budget for 1980 foresees such a reduction, but as a consequence of the expected slowdown in export growth, circumstances may be less favorable than in 1979.

reforms.description: The abolition of the investment tax, previously at 5 percent, is expected to increase incentives for investments in new capacity that align better with current cost structures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Eliminating the investment tax is designed to enhance investment incentives, thereby fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The abolition of the investment tax is expected to increase incentives for investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the investment landscape by removing a tax that could hinder new capacity investments.
reforms.source_quote: The abolition of the investment tax (previously at 5 percent) as from July 1979 incentives to invest in new capacity better adapted to present cost structures may have increased.
